documentation.content:
The migrationsTick scheduled function hasn't emitted its heartbeat in over 26 hours. It runs every 24h and now logs '[migrationsTick] heartbeat' on EVERY run (including no-op runs and runs skipped because another runner held the lease), so a >26h gap means the daily migration tick has silently stopped firing. Pending schema migrations would then never apply, and a half-applied migration would never get its retry.

Investigation:
  1. gcloud functions logs read migrationsTick --project nobigdeal-pro --gen2 --limit 50
  2. gcloud scheduler jobs list --project nobigdeal-pro | grep migrationsTick
  3. Read system/migrations (appliedVersion, lastError, lastFailedVersion) and system/migrations_lock (a stuck lease self-expires after 15m; if expiresAt is far in the future something is wrong).
  4. If a migration is failing, fix the script and let the next tick retry, or call runMigrations manually (admin only).
